column2: Phase 3 unstubs the audit-gate inference via Anthropic Messages API + native MCP connector
column3: Phase 3 unstubs the audit-gate inference. AuditGateDO.runAuditSession now calls Anthropic's Messages API at /v1/messages with anthropic-beta: mcp-client-2025-11-20 header, passing the persona's mcp_servers.operational as the native MCP connector array — for ams-canon-code-auditor this is [{type: url, url: https://oddkit.klappy.dev/mcp, name: oddkit}]. System prompt is the persona-shaped assembly from Phase 2 (oddkit_get resolution of system_prompt_uri). User message contains PR coordinates + the PR diff fetched inline from github.com/{owner}/{repo}/pull/{n}.diff (public-repo PoC; capped at 60KB) + an explicit reminder of the output contract. Model: claude-sonnet-4-5. Verdict extracted from text-type content blocks, mcp_tool_use/mcp_tool_result blocks ignored as intermediate work. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY added to Env interface and wrangler.toml secrets list — fail-fast with anthropic_api_key_not_configured if unset.

column2: PoC framing wins over phase ceremony — three-endpoint plan would have over-engineered the proof step
column3: PoC framing wins over phase ceremony. Operator called out the three-endpoint migration plan as drift ("/audit-gate-test → /audit-gate-canary → /audit-gate") risking forgotten cleanup. Considered redesign with single endpoint + GitHub Actions OIDC auth, but operator chose "It's a PoC. Let's just build it and test Production." Correct call — three-endpoint cleanup is a Phase 4 concern; the PoC's job is "does the substrate + persona resolution + MCP-wired inference actually produce a verdict in production." Phase 4 collapses endpoints when that's the next thing being tested.

column2: Persona mcp_servers.operational names are resolved to URLs via an allow-list in the runtime, not blindly forwarded
column3: When passing persona profile mcp_servers.operational to Anthropic's MCP connector, the short names (e.g. "oddkit") need URL resolution. PoC hardcodes oddkit → ODDKIT_MCP_URL constant. Phase 4+ may move URLs into the persona profile itself or resolve via an MCP registry. The current filter (.filter(name => name === "oddkit")) is intentionally restrictive — unknown names silently drop rather than crash, but also rather than blindly forwarding strings to Anthropic that could be attacker-controlled if the profile fetch is ever compromised. Substrate-side allow-list is the bottleneck for trust, not the profile.

column2: Anthropic MCP connector beta is mcp-client-2025-11-20 — verdict lives in text blocks, mcp_tool_* are intermediate
column3: Anthropic MCP connector beta is mcp-client-2025-11-20 (current as of 2026-05-12; mcp-client-2025-04-04 is deprecated). The header is anthropic-beta. Tools are accessed by the agent automatically — no client.beta.messages.create() abstraction needed, just the raw POST. Response content array contains mixed types: text, tool_use, mcp_tool_use, mcp_tool_result. The verdict lives in text blocks; the rest is intermediate. Workers fetch() to api.anthropic.com works directly with x-api-key header.

column2: PR diff fetched via public github.com .diff endpoint, capped at 60KB; private-repo + chunking are Phase 4
column3: PR diff fetched via github.com/{owner}/{repo}/pull/{n}.diff — the unauthenticated public diff endpoint. For private repos this fails; Phase 4+ adds a GITHUB_TOKEN env. Diff capped at 60KB chars to bound token consumption on huge PRs. Truncation marker is appended. Phase 4+ could chunk + iterate, or move diff fetching to an agent-side GitHub MCP tool so the agent decides what to look at.

column2: Phase 3 PR forthcoming; production test requires two secrets and a curl probe
column3: Phase 3 PR forthcoming. Six files: worker/src/runtime/audit-gate.ts (runAuditSession replaced — was 30-line stub, now ~95 lines real inference), worker/src/types.ts (+8 lines ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), worker/wrangler.toml (+6 lines secret doc), worker/src/index.ts (unchanged from main), worker/package.json (unchanged from main; no new deps — Anthropic API is plain fetch()), journal/2026-05-12-phase-3-anthropic-mcp-connector.tsv (this DOLCHEO trail). Local typecheck passes. Wrangler v4 dry-run produces 2.3MB bundle with all bindings. Production test requires: (a) wrangler secret put AMS_AUDIT_GATE_TEST_SECRET, (b) wrangler secret put ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, (c) curl probe against ams.klappy.dev/audit-gate-test with both the bearer secret and valid PR coordinates. Audit gate will auto-fire on the PR itself — first production self-audit of the runtime auditing the code that just unstubbed the runtime's inference. Recursion depth: maximum.
